title: Amber Lens Laboratory
description: The user operates a post-processing laboratory where the MosquitoInAmber specimen is viewed through the SunglassesKhronos lenses. Each lens acts as a viewport with independent post-processing effect chains that the user builds, reorders, and balances while maintaining a global luminance conservation law. Undo/redo lets the user experiment freely across both lens pipelines.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer from three/addons. Load SunglassesKhronos.glb centered at (0,1.5,0) facing the camera and MosquitoInAmber.glb at (0,0,-3) behind the glasses. Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,1.5,4) looking at origin. Add warm ambient light (0xffeedd, 0.4) and a directional light simulating a desk lamp from upper-right. Render the scene into two separate RenderTargets mapped onto the left and right lens meshes of the sunglasses (identify lens meshes by traversal, use their UVs). Each lens has an independent EffectComposer chain. Provide a bottom panel with 6 draggable effect cards: Bloom, ChromaticAberration, Sepia, FilmGrain, Vignette, HueSaturation. Users drag cards onto a left or right lens drop-zone to append that pass. Clicking a card already in a chain cycles its intensity through 3 levels (low/med/high). Right-clicking removes it. Users can reorder passes within a lane via drag. Implement a luminance conservation law: the sum of all intensity levels across BOTH lens chains must equal exactly 8. Display a luminance meter (0-16 range, target=8). When adding or increasing intensity would exceed 8, auto-reduce the oldest pass on the opposite lens. When removing would drop below 8, auto-increase the newest pass on the opposite lens. Implement full undo/redo (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y) storing snapshots of both chains and all intensities, supporting at least 20 history states. Show chain state as labeled pill lists above each lens. The amber specimen should slowly rotate on Y-axis so effects are visible through both lenses in real-time. Render the full scene behind the glasses normally, but each lens shows its processed version composited via ShaderMaterial sampling the respective RenderTarget. Minimum 10 meaningful interaction steps: add 3 effects per lens, reorder one, cycle intensity twice, remove one, undo twice, redo once.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  leftLensPassCount: number,         // count of post-processing passes active on left lens
  rightLensPassCount: number,         // count of post-processing passes active on right lens
  totalLuminanceUnits: number,         // sum of all intensity levels across both lens chains (target: 8)
  bloomIntensityLeft: number,         // bloom pass intensity on left lens (0=none, 1=low, 2=med, 3=high)
  bloomIntensityRight: number,         // bloom pass intensity on right lens (0=none, 1=low, 2=med, 3=high)
  sepiaIntensityLeft: number,         // sepia pass intensity on left lens (0=none, 1=low, 2=med, 3=high)
  vignetteIntensityRight: number,         // vignette pass intensity on right lens (0=none, 1=low, 2=med, 3=high)
  chromaticAberrationActive: boolean,         // whether chromatic aberration is active on either lens
  luminanceBalanced: boolean,         // whether total luminance equals exactly 8 units
  lastDraggedEffect: string,         // name of the most recently dragged effect card
  autoReductionTriggered: boolean,         // whether automatic intensity reduction occurred to maintain conservation law
  reorderOperationCount: number,         // count of pass reordering operations performed within lens chains
}
